title: Max Heapify Incorrect Comparison Bug Fix

statement.markdown: Given an array of integers, implement a function to heapify a max heap by swapping the largest element at a given index with its largest child (if it exists). However, your function contains a logial bug that results in incorrect heap properties.
statement.inputFormat: Input will be an array of integers and an index representing the position to heapify.
statement.outputFormat: The function should return the heapified array.
statement.notes: Assumptions: The input array is not empty and indices are 0-based.

examples.input: Example input (matching the stdin format that main wrapper expects)

hints.text: Check the comparison logic in the heapify function.

editorial.title: Max Heapify Incorrect Comparison Bug Fix
editorial.intuition: Max heapify involves swapping the root with its largest child (if it exists) to maintain heap properties.
editorial.optimalSolution: A simple iterative solution can achieve this by finding the largest child and swapping it with the root if necessary.

functionSignature.cpp: void heapify(int arr[], int n, int i);
functionSignature.java: void heapify(int[] arr, int n, int i);
functionSignature.python: def heapify(self, arr: list[int], n: int, i: int) -> None:
